question: While making an HTTP Webhook connector executable, which statement about its configuration is FALSE?

options.text: When no HMAC scopes are specified, the default scope is `BODY` and the signature is generated from the body of the webhook request.
An Activation Condition like `=(request.body.status = "OK")` triggers the webhook only when the inbound body's `status` field equals `"OK"`.
For Basic authorization, you must base64-encode the Username and Password yourself before entering them into the template properties.
Variable Mapping uses a FEEL expression such as `={ myDocumentId: request.body.id }` to project payload fields onto process variables.

a.text: True per the docs: `BODY` is listed as the default HMAC scope, and the signature data is generated from the body of the webhook request when only `BODY` (or empty) is set. So this is not the false statement.

b.text: True per the docs: leaving the Activation Condition empty triggers the webhook every time; setting `=(request.body.status = "OK")` triggers it only when the body's `status` is `"OK"`. So this is not the false statement.

c.text: This is the false statement. The docs say explicitly: "Provide the values in plain text, not base64-encoded." The runtime base64-encodes the credentials when validating the incoming `Authorization: Basic ...` header.

d.text: True per the docs: Variable Mapping uses a FEEL Result Expression such as `={ myDocumentId: request.body.id }` to extract payload fields into process variables. So this is not the false statement.

explanation: Per the Webhook connector docs, the Username and Password for Basic authorization must be provided in plain text — the runtime handles base64-encoding them when comparing against the inbound `Authorization: Basic ...` header. Manually base64-encoding the values would cause the comparison to fail. The other three statements (default HMAC `BODY` scope, FEEL Activation Condition syntax, FEEL Variable Mapping syntax) match the documented behavior.
